title: 2014 RFL Championship
wikitext:
{{Short description|none}} {{Infobox rugby football league season |title = 2014 RFL Championship |league = [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] |pixels = 216px |caption = The Kingstone Press Championship logo |duration = 26 Rounds (Followed by 4 round playoffs) |no_of_teams = 14 |highest_attendance = |lowest_attendance = |avg_attendance = |attendance = |TV = [[Premier Sports]] |season = 2014 Season |season_champs = |season_champ_name = Grand final winners |league_leaders = |league_leaders_name = League Leaders Shield |second_place = |MVP = |MVP_link = |top try scorer_link = |promote = |promoted_from = |relegate = |relegate_to = [[RFL League 1|Championship 1]] |playoffs = |playoffs_link = |conf1 = |conf1_link = |conf1_champ = |conf1-runner-up = |conf2 = |conf2_link = |conf2_champ = |conf2-runner-up = |playoffs_MVP = |playoffs_MVP_link = |finals = |finals_link = |finals_champ = |finals-runner-up = |finals_MVP = |finals_MVP_link = |prevseason_link = 2013 RFL Championship |prevseason_year = 2013 |nextseason_link = 2015 RFL Championship |nextseason_year = 2015 }} The '''2014 Rugby Football League Championship''', known as the '''Kingstone Press Championship''' due to sponsorship reasons, was a semi-professional [[rugby league|rugby league football]] competition played in the UK, one tier below the first tier [[Super League]]. The 2014 season was the second and final season to consist of a 14-team division following the expansion of the league in [[2013 RFL Championship|2013]].{{cite news|title=Relegation is scrapped from Championship this campaign|url=http://www.keighleynews.co.uk/sport/9535699.print/|access-date=4 June 2012|newspaper=Keighley News|date=16 February 2012}} Relegation to [[RFL League 1|Championship 1]] was in place for the 2014 season, along with the play-offs with the format for the play-offs remaining the same with no promotion to the [[Super League]]. With two [[Super League]] teams relegated in [[Super League XIX|2014]] and the Championship reduced to 12 teams in 2015, as part of the reform of the leagues, five teams were relegated at the end of the regular season with one team promoted at the end of [[2014 Championship 1]].{{cite news|title=Kingstone Press Championships clubs prepare for kick off|url=http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/29455/kingstone-press-championships-clubs-prepare|access-date=20 February 2014|newspaper=Rugby League Championships|date=10 February 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20140225021430/http://www.therfl.co.uk/news/article/29455/kingstone-press-championships-clubs-prepare|archive-date=25 February 2014|url-status=dead}} ==Teams== The competition featured 12 of the 14 teams from [[2013 RFL Championship|2013]] plus the Champions and Play-off winner of the [[2013 Championship 1]] season, which were [[North Wales Crusaders]] and [[Rochdale Hornets]]. [[Hunslet R.L.F.C.|Hunslet Hawks]] and [[York City Knights]], who finished in the bottom two in 2013 were relegated into [[RFL League 1|Championship 1]]. {| class="wikitable" style="font-size: 85%" |- ! colspan=2|Legend |- | style="background:#ff9; width:15px;"|   |Reigning champions |- | style="background:#4682b4; width:15px;"|   |Defending [[Northern Rail Cup]] Champions |- | style="background:#CCFFCC; width:15px;"|   |Promoted |} {| class="wikitable sortable" style="width:95%; font-size:95%;" |- ! style="width:15px;" class="unsortable"| ! Competition points: For win = 3; For draw = 2; For loss by 12 points or fewer = 1.{{cite web|title=Points System|url=http://www.rugbyleaguechampionships.co.uk/championship/points_system|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130208200244/http://www.rugbyleaguechampionships.co.uk/championship/points_system|url-status=dead|archive-date=8 February 2013|publisher=Rugby League Championships|access-date=27 January 2013}} }} ==Season Results== {{main|2014 RFL Championship season results}} The regular league season saw the 14 teams play each other twice (one home, one away) over 26 matches. The top eight teams at the end of the regular season went through to the play-offs to determine the winners of the Championship. ==See also== * [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] * [[2014 Championship 1]] * [[British rugby league system]] * [[Super League]] * [[Rugby League Conference]] * [[Northern Ford Premiership]] * [[National League Cup (rugby)|National League Cup]] ==References== {{Reflist}} ==External links== *[https://web.archive.org/web/20160305025245/http://www.rugbyleaguechampionships.co.uk/championship/ Official Website] {{2014 in Rugby League}} {{RFL Championship}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Rfl Championship}} [[Category:2014 in English rugby league]] [[Category:2014 in Welsh rugby league]] [[Category:Rugby Football League Championship|2014]]
